content:
The steam from the last batch of cheong fun is still clinging to my hair, but for the first time in fifteen years, I don’t reach for the towel to wipe the condensation off the glass. I let the droplets run, tracing erratic paths through the flour-dust, distorting the view of 7th Avenue. 

It is Friday afternoon. The prep tables are wiped down, but the large plastic bins of grated daikon are missin
